channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 69 replies — 276.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:27 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62226196
>>62224749
>9 years of bagholding
Hey cuckold, check the price :)

62226241
>>62226196
thx i just checked oh boy! am I le excite!

62226533
>*dumps*

62227022
can I get an honest and unbiased QRD on link?

62227259
>>62227022
No, I don't think you can. Read the white papers and think for yourself. That'll probably be the closest you can get. Everyone else who would answer you is emotionally invested one way or the other. Me? I think it's the only project that matters and that it'll make me the equivalent of a billionaire one day. It's the next iteration of the internet, just as DLT was the next iteration of the computer.

62227290
>>62227022
No.

62227293
>>62224577
>dumps to single digits

62227302
>>62224749
Bag size?

62227385
>>62227022
Fundamentally, I’ve noticed a lot of new posters in the last 69hr (SIXTY NINE) asking about Chainlink and just wanted to warn you. Chainlink is shilled HEAVILY here and it’s very hard to understand so this background is crucial to ensure you stay safe.

Basically, a bunch of RACIST WHITE CIS MALE 4channers tried to force it as /ourcoin/, during the presale ico phase of chainlink there was a minimum requirement of 300eth to enter the presale.

Coin continued to get shilled and pumped up and hyped for the sibios event that link was attended, whole event turned out to be a flop chainlink had a presentation in a room of like 18 people (IT WAS BESIDE THE FUCKING TOILETS FOR FUCK'S SAKES, YOU COULD HEAR THEM FLUSHING), literally no news or partnership came from the event.

Now during this alt bull run lots of anons and took advantage of this and shilling this coin to all the new money and newfags that joined in december and don't know this story.
The coin is HEAVILY manipulated by a literal TRANNY DISCORD who pump and dump this coin more than their boyfriends pump and dump hot cum into their asses, and the supply is dried up from huge fucking chink whales (xi jinping is rumoured to hold link) who accumulated below ICO price to create a artificially lower supply.

In regards to actual project that chainlink aiming to achieve it's nothing more than a basic json parser (OH MY GOD ITS JSON PARSER...) for smart contracts (LOL HOW CAN A CONTRACT BE "SMART"???), would take like a day to add to ethereum by itself.. literally making links whole concept pointless and definitely no need for a token. You're literally just buying a wh*tepaper.

They only have 2 devs who are literal pajeets who are payed in big macs (non beef) and Surgay NAZAJEET is a literal psychologist (trusting a shrink? is he gonna tell us to go clean our room next?). So FUNDAMENTALLY, it's a scam, ok thanks.

Now go do the needful and fundamentally buy a real coin with fundamentals like XRP.

62227397
>>62227385
I can't tell whether gatekeeping normies is good or evil. I go back and forth

62227410
>>62226079
Me too Son, just a little later, a-and besides 10 years is nothing . Just hold on WGMI

62227441
>>62227397
Normies already live on easy mode, we live on nightmare difficulty.

62227473
>>62224616
bad, sell the news event + token not needed
>>62224627
chainlink might matter, LINK does not

>>62224974
many ICO bros made it back in 2020 at $50 withtheir 50-70k stacks, sure they're no warrent buffets but it was enough to live comfortably, too bad most didn't sell

>>62225125
yeah, as long as the tokens aren't needed you can have top of the NASDAQ sucking Chainlinks dick, people just stopped caring, it's not 2017 anymore. Back then it would've sent LINk to triple digits, but those days are long gone, peoeple wanna see actual demand nowadays, but it ain't coming, because Chainlink /= LINK

62227498
>>62224863
Sui has always been 1k

62227508
>>62227498
jesus dude that's like $10k, even if LINK does a 3x (and that's being generous) you'd only have $30k, what are you gonna do with that? lol

62227538
SERGEY BETRAYS US

SELL SELL FOR YOUR LIVES

62227556
>>62224958
This anon is correct.

62227563
>>62227508
Link will be 1k+

62227593
>>62227563
No it will pump to $30 and dump back down to $5.

62229154
>>62224577
it's big news!

62229156
>>62229154

62229158
>>62229156

62229164
>>62229158
Is that fat bitch* still fudding?

*the woman not Sergey

62229165
>>62229164
She's Nadine Chakar from the DTCC and she's partners with Chainlink

62232247
Bump. They trying to slide the other thread so hard lol.

62232345
>>62227563
lmao
lmao
lmao
lmao

62232462
>>62232345
Swift, DTCC, Fidelity plus the other 600 more.

62232545
>>62224577
how tf did we get to $50 on nothing but with literally everything we are barely afloat at $10?

62232606
>>62232545
because when it hit $50 everything else in crypto was pumping for no reason at all, leading to a terrible crash, and the market has basically been dead for 6 years, with only bitcoin seeing consistent gains and solana's shitcoin casino being lamer and scammier than eth's. When accounting for inflation, this entire market has been on life support since 2021.

62232658
I'm so excited for you crazy kids

item.title: Circumcision reparations — 63 replies — 252.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Circumcision reparations

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:26 UTC

OP

All men who were circumcised as babies deserve at least 10 million USA dollars.
They were innocent babies and were horribly, permanently and irreversibly mutilated for their entire one life on earth before it even began.
There is NO way to FULLY or even MOSTLY recover their lost foreskins and all of it's structures. Stretching does not nearly even come close to restoring it to it's original form.

>Why at least 10 million usa dollars?

Because there was a case of a adult man who merely had some mean words, drawings and jokes said to him at work. Nothing criminally illegal like death threats, leaking his social security number etc. He later quit his job at Tesla he wasn't fired. He was offered 15 million 2022 dollars for his hurt feelings by a usa judge https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/tesla-racism-lawsuit-worker-rejects-15-million-payout-rcna34655
He was literally offered 15 million dollars that the government would take from Tesla and hand directly to him. He rejected it because he wanted more then 15 million 2022 dollars but the point stands the government is willing to say mean words and hurt feelings are worth at least 15 million 2022 dollars.
Being circumcised as a baby is AT LEAST ten times worse so a mere ten million dollars per mutilated at birth man is already a deep deep compromise.

Early replies (first 3)

62230647
>>62230645
CASE #2

https://www.usatoday.com/story/money/2023/07/20/mcdonalds-lawsuit-hot-chicken-nugget/70436203007/

A random family is in a car. These are not employees they're just random customers.
They go to McDonalds drive through

Mom buys nuggets

Gives nuggets (corpses of tortured and mutilated animals that were factory farmed) to her little kid

Kid drops nugget on thigh and it sits on her thigh for a few minutes or so while mother is distracted.

It leaves a slight burn on thigh

Burn heals completely leaving a small barely noticable and not painful scar on thigh

Girl gets 800,000 dollars.

Circumcising a baby is at least 13 times worse easily.
It's obviously way way worse than 13 times but whatever let's be extremely conservative

That's just over 10 million usa dollars

You deserve this.

ROUTINE CIRCUMCISION OF BABY BOYS IS BATTERY!! It is non consensual touching which is medically unnecessary. This is actually legally correct at least in usa as intactivist lawyers have argued

GUESS What? A battery can be worth 5 million dollars or more

Circumcision is doing severe permanent irreversible physical damage to an innocent baby boy . Possible severe mental damage too

A hedge fund executive punched a parking attendant once during a dispute over a car.

62230660
>>62230647
The Injuries: A broken cheekbone and a concussion. The victim recovered physically within months, but sued for emotional distress and battery.

The Verdict: The jury awarded $1.2 million for medical bills and pain, but added $7 million in punitive damages specifically because of the defendant’s wealth and the "predatory" nature of the assault.

This is the classic "regular guy" example often cited in legal circles.

The Incident: A man was punched once by a security guard at a Las Vegas nightclub.

The Injuries: A broken jaw and a concussion. No permanent disability, but the victim required surgery (his jaw was wired shut for weeks).

The Result: A jury awarded him $4.75 million.

$5 Million (Carroll v. Trump)

While there was a later $83 million verdict for defamation, the original 2023 verdict for the battery itself was $5 million.

The Incident: A single encounter in a department store dressing room.

The Injuries: No "super severe" physical injuries (like broken bones or hospitalization), but the jury focused on the psychological trauma.

The Result: The $5 million award was for the act of battery and the associated pain and suffering.

If Carroll can get 5 million then so should men circumcised as boys

Not everyone would instantly become a multimillionaire No circumcision reparations for women

Tens of millions of men would be given millions of dollars printed into existence. Yes it would cause inflation. That's okay. BRING THE DIGITAL CURRENCY OR TEN THOUSAND DOLLAR USA BILLS AND ONE HUNDRED THOUSAND DOLLARS USA BILLS NOW

100 USA DOLLAR BILL WON'T EVEN BUY YOU A SODA AND THAT IS OKAY

If bread costs 10,000 dollars a loaf that's fine. It's the uncircumcised who didn't get reparations who won't be able to afford bread at first. They can work for a circumcised person who got reparations for 15,000 dollars an hour or something

https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=WWVz_DcSeZc

62230666
>>62230645
Consider yourself lucky your dick doesnt stink. Its like having a vagina as a man. And thats coming from someone uncut.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231518
>>62230677
INDIAN DETECTED
GOOD MORNING SAR

62231539
there's not enough money in the world

62231578
>>62231425
Yes im very familiar with this
It’s good for preventing new circumcisions from happening but im not sure it will help with reparations for those already cut

62231585
>>62231539
Print it into existence. Same way they do for endless wars in the Middle East for Israel

62231605
>>62231585
I want more than money for reparations

62231618
>>62231605
I know but it should be realistic too
I think many doctors are guilty of rape for practicing infant circumcision especially certain cartoonishly evil forms of it like baby pp sucking

62231631
Wait what is so bad about circumcision again? It's one small piece of skin...

62231636
>>62231631
I don’t believe you’re asking that in good faith

62231645
>>62230666
Satan speaks.
Circumcision started because men started having major dick infection from never washing their dick.
>>62231631
The modern version cuts off more skin then the original one.
Now it leads to health issues and deformations since they cut off the entire skin.

62231712
>>62230645
You don't want to be uncircumsised. Masturbation feels so good that it's a legitimate addiction.

62231738
>>62231712
All the more reason I must either become a multimillionaire or play Minecraft PvP with the evil players on the server

62231768
>>62231636
Nah I'm actually ignorant to the convo on this. Haven't had any kids yet but planning on it. Just read up some on it. I don't know that I buy that getting it done creates any issues but it seems pointless to get it done these days and just one of those things everyone in the US does because that's what they've always done.

62231787
>>62231768
>>62231768
It seems to do mental damage and it also reduces pleasure from sex and masturbation, foreskin acts as natural lubricant and also helps prevent the pp head from being keratinized / dried out so it’s like if a cut pp constantly has coconut oil applied to it without needing anything applied. Theres a bunch of other smallish benefits that add up

62231804
>>62230645
after becoming sexually active my foreskin has stretched too much and now i need circumcision, otherwise i get yeast infection

62231819
>>62231787
Damn. Thanks mom and dad. The boomers in the US had it done to us no questions asked.

I think in the US the mental part might actually be the other way. A kid might catch some locker room teasing and/or girls gossiping about him if he's uncut. But they're prob whores so who cares

62231831
Here's the shit I'm talking about in the US: https://www.reddit.com/r/AskWomenNoCensor/comments/1bnt2gi/how_common_is_it_that_women_make_fun_of_or_talk/

This would be my one reservation for a future son. I know it's plebbit but still.

62231938
>>62231831
American women are mean so what? First of all if shes a virgin his dick is the first she will see
Second of all he can get a woman from overseas who is from a majority uncut country

62231944
>>62231804
the cutting you are talking about is probably less invasive and removes less structures then routine infant circumcision

Also look into alternatives to surgery like steroids and whatnot

62231994
>>62230895
My penis is fully functional, and foreskin is not "half of your dick" either. If all I'm missing is extra addiction to pussy and sex "feels" plus dick cheese, then I'm glad I got circumcised indeed, wouldn't want sex and women to have any more control over my life than they already do.

62232027
>>62231994
That’s just coping. Someone could use the same argument to justify removing children’s tastebuds
They’ll still have their tongue and be able to speak properly but they’ll just no longer need to scrape their tongue for 10 seconds once or twice a day in order to remove any plaque on it. Plus they will have no ability to or a reduced ability to taste food which will reduce obesity and result in more healthy but bad tasting foods being eaten more often.

Tell me the symmetry breaker in my analogy or be honest and bite the Bullet and concede that this type of procedure done to babies would also be justified

62232320
Bim

62232353
Americans are a strange, weird breed indeed.

62232370
>>62232027
You are gay. If removing taste buds made you less likely to put cocks in your mouth, I would be all for it. You. Are. Gay.

62232399
>>62232353
I know
I am technically American because I was born here but I hate it here

62232413
>>62232399
You have to go back

62232432
>>62232413
troll

62232631
>>62232432
Tongue my balls faggot. I tithe to my church. I tip 20% minimum. I give to charity. I volunteer at the animals shelter. What do you? Besides suck dicks?

62232642
>>62232631
What charities? What church? Does the animal shelter have mostly dogs and cats in it and are those pets fed a diet of mostly the bodies of other animals that were tortured in factory farms?

62232647
>>62232642

62232655
>>62232647

item.title: >never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak… — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak…

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:25 UTC

OP

>never had a gf
>never been on a date
>now 36
>had jobs
>go to gym
>finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25
>make multiple dating profiles on different apps
>zero matches

So I'm a confirmed subhuman. And, judging by the responses to ugly males who post their pics on Reddit and ask why they get no matches, normies openly laugh at me and think I deserve a low status life of slavery.

Why should I give a fuck about putting in effort at work or saving the west or whatever? Any sort of participation is a scam for someone like me.

Early replies (first 3)

62223666
>>62223664
>>>/adv/

62223769
>>62223664
depends how tall u are and if u have any real ugliness causing things like deformity or burn scars etc.

U probably are just average looking and if u arent 5'4'' u will find a gf

62224072
>>62223664
are you swiping on fat women or only the stacies?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224114
Plapjak won.

62224205
>>62223664
I doesn't have to be this way. You can get a passport and airplane ticket.

62224248
>>62223664
>36
>looked like shit
>expects matches on dating apps
What exactly have you been doing for the last 18 years of your adult life? At least do a trade or become a truck driver if (I assume) you didn't go to college. Are you seriously this fucking retarded?

62227684
Many such cases (Including me).

62227768
Post physique. You are probably unironically still fat as fuck/skinnyfat and likely balding, and sub-5 facially. Post physique and face you have nothing to lose.

62227796
>>62223664
you can think of being good with women as compound interest, the more you do it, the more you get

we also have compounded, but on the debt side

it's over

62231723
>>62224072
this. Dudes will be 5/10 at best swiping on stacies and wonder why they're single.

62231869
>>62223664
what you don't know yet is that having access to women will still make you feel empty. Believe me, I've fucked 50+ different women in the last two years. Even tried to have relationshits with some of them. None were worth my time and peace. You're better off finding rewarding activities that you truly enjoy.

62231876
You had a huge chunk of your youth years before dating apps were even a thing and you never tried asking a woman out?

62231883
>>62231723
Why is the presumption usually that you are either super picky, or not even trying, or don't work on yourself, or don't leave the house, things like that?
Might be true for a handful but not the majority.

62231914
Demoralization thread

62231917
>>62231876
I listened to the "dude chill don't worry it will happen" meme

Of course, nothing ever happened

62231922
>>62223664
Could be worse. You could be Indian or Pakistani.

62232507
>>62231883
>super picky
yeah it's dudes who are average going for stacies

>not even trying/not working on yourself
If an average guy doesn't try then he'll get 0 guaranteed. Only works for top 10% looking dudes. Maybe some whale will hit on you at Walmart or something if lucky.

>don't leave the house
this is ok if you use apps. It just comes down to exposure.

62232510
>>62223664
date fat ugly women. some of the fattest and ugliest people i know are married.

62232514
>>62231883
Why do you think it's not the majority of forever alones?

62232527
>>62223664
Literally get involved in other activities to meet people, dating apps are manufactured to be as soul crushing as possible.

62232535
>>62223664
I gave up and trooned out in 2017. I now make money dating rich men. If you're born a sub 6' autistic man post 1990 it's over, just give up.

62232551
>>62223664

Someone else's opinion should not become your reality. You are the master of your ship the captain of your soul, the blacksmith of your fortune. Go and make it as most do, treading through shark infested waters ignoring critics who care only to see you below them.
You've lost weight, now work on other aspects ffs! Wipe your tears and good luck. May we meet you here again when you'll post about your success!

62232652
>>62223664
>make multiple dating profiles on different apps
Buddy, dating apps no longer matches people. They seek profit, and they do that by baiting you into buying their subscription and then leave you in the ditch.

item.title: CLARITY ACT PASSED — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
CLARITY ACT PASSED

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:24 UTC

OP

CLARITY ACT PASSED

Early replies (first 3)

62231538
NO WAY

62231541
>>62231457
It hasn't even gone to senate yet retard

62231557
>>62231457
It passed a committee's approval, retard.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231575
>>62231557
Next stop is the actual floor my nigger so this is huge

62231591
>>62231575
Just like your next step is being on the floor with something huge

62231681
>>62231591
Your mom is quite large

62231687
>>62231457

To the moon

62231715
>>62231681
i've been lurking in this thread since he made that post to make sure you didn't miss the easiest Your Mom layup of all time

thank you

62231721
bearish

62231732
techno feudalism ftw

62231747
>>62231457
who cares
t. Bitcoin

62232387
>>62231457
Cool narrative, cycles in control

62232401
>>62231541
>>62231557
it already passed the house. it passed the senate committee with bipartisan support. so you can take a wild guess what will happen when it goes to the senate and then back to the house a second time.

62232407
>>62232401
We are gonna be so fucking rich my nigger

62232414
>>62232401
So it's destined to fail?
Guess I'm selling everything then :(

62232429
>>62231687
Based retard

62232493
>>62231457
What does this actually mean for crypto? Are we gonna flip or flop

62232627
>>62231457
They're doing this so their super AI can be better predictive of the market

62232634
>>62232493
Jews are selling the news but I think it’s a fake out.

62232646
>>62232493
Means the keys for defi are handed over to big banks. Congratulations on this, really.

item.title: XMR — 24 replies — 59.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
XMR

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:09 UTC

OP

Delisted at 160, currently at 410

Early replies (first 3)

62213668
Nobody gives two shits about crapto in general. You are a baggie trying to find a greater fool, you were the greatest fool

62213696
>>62213668
You are cattle.

62213702
>>62213696
No, I am cashflow that is never going to have a demand for your bags. No matter what you baggies advertise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214009
>>62213668
I made it through XMR, it's an excellent opportunity for anyone who listens.

62216189
I like the coin

62216387
>>62214009
Same I dumped on newbaggies when we hit 800. Years of accoomoolating paid off.

62216481
>>62213665
Yeah but what can I buy with it? Where do I sell it for fiat?

62217118
>>62216481
Check monerica, you can buy pretty much anything.

62217182
>>62216481
>Yeah but what can I buy with it?
Anything. I'm currently buying work supplies for my business with XMR. That compounded with how much I've made my business is basically free and I'm making a couple thousand dollars a week.

62222692
>>62214009
based
sound money, safe mode

62222705
>>62217182
based
I've been shilling it in Lebanon, and trying to educate everyone
fuck central banks
fuck fiat
(((keynesians))) deserve the rope

https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20210601-lebanon-crisis-among-world-s-worst-since-1850s-world-bank

never forget

62222769
>>62222705
Glad to see you're still around

62222781
>>62222769
thank you my friend
we have a saying here, roughly translates to:
if God is with us, who can be against us

62222813
>>62222781
Lovely, could've come straight from the apodeipnon

62223307
>>62222705
Fuck Israel too

62223522
How do I buy this dogshit and bury it in a ledger?
There was the wagyu hyperliquid thing but I don't trust that nigger

62223640
>>62223307
based

if you got some extra XMR to send I wouldn't mind
>>62214009
only asking cause I saw this
>>62222813
Christ is king

62224485
>>62223742
When we hit 500 I'll send 0.1 XMR, screenshot this

62226010
I like the coin

62226990
>>62224485
thank you friend

62227005
newfags still haven't learned what a monero spike means

62229871
>>62223742
No talents to sell?

62232296
>Delisted at 160, currently at 410
(*)(75 adjusted for Bitcoin)

62232605
>>62227005
Tell us

item.title: Day 1 of snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000 — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1 of snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:22 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62231434
>>62231412
Where the fuck is moonman? That's all I want to know, and what's his 2026 take on ICP. I've been all-in on ICP since the day moonman blessed us with his alpha, so fuckin, just tell me. If there's anyway of getting into his email list, how do we do it?

62231445
>>62231434
I don’t know who this moon man is, I just know that ICP has superior tech and is the only blockchain in the crypto space that actually rivals amazon web services. Good things take time and patience.

62231464
>>62231445
How can you be an ICP baggie and now know the legendary moonman, and his influence over /biz/ in the glorious '21 bullrun. Dude. You're clueless, hit the archives, the only reason I hold ICP is because moonman said all the right shit back in the day.
> ICP has superior tech
> only blockchain in the crypto space that actually rivals amazon web services

These and many other classic ICP talking points are all derived from the threads moonman made 5 years ago. The entire narrative was weaved by moonman, and all the shit you read today is nothing more than an echo of what we've been promised back in '21
If you're gonna be an icy piss baggie, learn the history newfag, you don't know the half of it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231483
>>62231412
You didn't even make it to day 10 last time

62231521
>>62231464
>These and many other classic ICP talking points
They are not talking points, I am an analyst. ICP has the technology, they are far more advanced than they were in 2021. in 2021 ICP didn’t have chain fusion, it didn’t have partnerships, it didn’t have a cloud engine, it didn’t have caffeine.

You are a fucking moron. Kill yourself.

62231611
>>62231521
I'm an ICP maxxxi you dumb hick son of a bitch, I'm more of an icypee enjoyer than you. Fuck you, you don't even know about the most important part of ICP (moonman) and you wanna talk shit to me. lil nigga I'm one of the big bois, shut your ass up and listen to your betters. Go into the archives and inform your stupid ass

62231879
>>62231611
I DONT FUCKING KNOW WHO THAT IS, I DON’T CARE ABOUT SOME FUCKING INFLUENCER, FUCK YOU AND FUCK MOONMAN

62231905
>>62231879
how could you not know moonman? are you some kind of retard? look him up on tiktok dumbass dont u know how to do DD?

62231987
>>62231879
He's not an influencer you fucking retard, moonman was a /biz/ tripfag legend. What the fuck are you doing on 4chan? What the fuck are you doing on my website if you don't understand anything about anything that happens here. You're a dirty ESL thirdie son of a whore if you're here without having any knowledge of these things. Eat shit, sell your bags, get some LINK or XRP, those are a little more up your speed you subhuman Indiian mongrel

62232035
>>62231987
I speak 4 languages and I am whiter than you.

I don’t like jeets. I don’t like niggers and I don’t like your attitude.

62232109
>>62231434
When moonman shilled this, the price was $60, now it's $2.8, saying you were here since moonman means you are down 99% LMAO

62232146
>>62232109
It doesn't mean he was wrong just early. This will be $10k when the market realize the potential of this with datacenters. The it won't matte lr if ur entry was $100 or $1. Literally any time is the best time to buy this. It's a lock and a steal at current price. You'll be a millionaire if u buy now

62232188
>>62232146
Nah it's over, they had 5 years to produce something and aside from dumping tokens dfinity had nothing to show for it, tech is not everything, VHS beat the laserdisk even though the laserdisk was superior

62232641
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 40 replies — 48.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-05-14 21:44 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224145
bump. y'all make posts about wanting an easy way out but no one puts in the work. ive been trying to make something work for years at this point and i feel like im on the cusp of something, but i realize what im looking for is outside my knowledge. i aint smart enough for this. ive tried so many methods but im hitting dead ends. i need the help of someone who's smarter than me here

62224160
>>62224145
I'm working on the same thing but I have no interest in helping anyone, why would I do that?

62224188
>>62224160
Fair point. The way I see it, I don’t mind if you know the strategy, especially if you end up helping me. That’s the least I could do.

My goal isn’t to milk it forever. I just want to use it to reach a point where I can retire and live a simple, happy life. After that, whatever happens to the strategies or lines doesn’t really matter to me. If you wanted to keep using them and scale them further, I’d be fine with that.

You mentioned you were working on something similar. How far did you get with it? What kind of metrics or performance were you seeing?

62224200
>>62219625
post pseudo code

62224207
>>62224200
the strategies are a mixture of VWAP and ORB. i used a base idea and iterated from there, testing different rule sets and requirements before deciding on the current setup. do you have a background in statistics or quantitative analysis?

62224225
>>62223860
holding for seconds or minutes at a time is usually a scalper. if you're holding for 30 mins to hours+ i dont think 1:1RR is efficient, there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing and achieve the same reward.

clearly you have some type of edge. you could either trade your own capital in MNQ contracts or try to make the drawdowns fit for the prop firm plan. you dont have to trade full size NQs you could have a lot more flexibility with MNQ.

>strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones
this is where you have to drill down to every trade taken and qualitatively sort out the lower quality trades. theres certainly nuances and details that differ across the trades taken so finding a better way to filter out the bad ones should yield better efficiency. you cant do that type of work through top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

62224267
>>62224188
I have it working, it was running live for a few weeks. Made me an average 1% per day, but upon the first sign of danger I panicked and turned it off. Working on retooling it to notify me and I can configure it on the fly.

62224277
>>62224225

>there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing

Well anon, that’s basically the core problem. I don’t know how to qualitatively filter out lower-quality trades.

I’ve tried pulling data at the exact point of entry, things like EntryFromOpenPct, EntryFromVWAPPct, Prior10RangePct, Prior30ChurnPct, DayRangeSoFarPct, ES confirmation, EMA state, signal candle body/range, stop distance, target distance, planned RR, and a few other metrics.

The idea was to see if winners or losers had anything consistent in common, then build some kind of grading system to decide before entry whether a trade was worth taking. But that ended up being a dead end too. Although I am still testing variants of that as we speak with harsher filters, I'm not super hopeful.

I also don’t want to rely entirely on ChatGPT or AI to generate ideas, because it could easily lead me astray and I wouldn’t know, since I’m out of my element here. That’s why I’m stuck.

>you don’t have to trade full-size NQs, you could have more flexibility with MNQ

I looked into that too. The issue is that if I size down enough to survive the drawdowns, my scale becomes too small. It would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.

And once I run the math, I’d be spending 3–5 hours a day trading for basically nothing. Not even $100/day in actual profit after costs and resets.

So technically, I do have a path to payouts right now. The problem is the math says it’s just not worth it.

>top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

Sometimes if I have an idea, I’ll manually backtest it through a few days or weeks first. If it shows promise, then I’ll programmatically test it across the full dataset.

There are definitely some strategies that work well in specific regimes. The problem is that by the time you realize the regime has shifted, you’ve usually already given back most or all of what you made from it.

62224392
>>62224277
that sounds like the right path to be taking to find that extra quality filter. maybe you need more testing time to find that last filter piece. i've learned that more criteria is not always yielding more efficient results though, you can end up filtering out alot of good trades and reducing trade volume too much. i dont think AI is good for idea generation for trading or signals, personally i use AI to run all the math stats and simulations exclusively.

>would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.
I passed most evaluations in 1 week to 8 weeks. i feel like if you're trying to pass in less than a week your size is prob too big. the 1 week pass was during a highly volatile period where i got much bigger wins.

It seems like you're on the right track. in trading, usually just doing 1 or 2 things really really well is better than having too many tools. i still disagree with your core stats of 1:1RR and requirements of high win-rate. i know theres traders that make that work but i've always wanted 1 big win to pay for 3+ losers. the big fish arent always biting but when you land 1 you realize chasing small fish all the time is a waste.

62224555
>>62224392
Everything you mentioned: sizing down, different contract sizes, different RR models, higher RR/lower win rate systems, and higher win rate/lower RR systems… I’ve already considered, tested, or looked into.

The main issue is the math. If it takes me around five weeks to pass a typical $50,000 evaluation with a $3,000 profit target, that works out to about $120 per trading day. If I then use the same strategy on a funded account, which makes sense since the rules are usually very similar, that same strategy is still only producing about $120 per day. After the prop firm split and taxes, that is well under $100 per day, which is not really viable long term or enough for financial independence.

The only way to make that work would be running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate. If the strategy hits a deeper drawdown period, I could lose all of them at once.

So the issue is that I either size down so much that I spend thousands on evals, resets, and activation fees just to withdraw a few hundred dollars later, or I need to find a strategy that can grow more aggressively.

That is why I’m focused on a higher win rate + a higher profit factor. A lower win rate strategy might still have good EV on paper, but it also means more losing streaks and more resets along the way. A higher win rate gives me a better chance to build a cushion before those rough periods come.

With that being said, I’m not going to pretend I know everything. After all, I’m the one who came to this board asking for help.

The truth is, I don’t currently have a strategy with the kind of stats you’re describing (3:1RR) The one I have now, with its current win rate and profit factor, is the best I’ve been able to build so far hence why I’ve been sticking with it.

62224557
>>62224267
1% a day means 500% returns after just half a year. If you got a strategy like that then you’d be flying in a private jet within a few years lmao

62224754
>>62224555
>running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate.
most prop firms let you have multiple accounts and if the economics are good enough for 1 you should always max the number of accounts for better scale. other prop firms just give you a bigger account (100k or 250k). yeah averaging $100 per day for 250 trading days per year is only $25k but ideally you get to 5x scale within the first 2 months then thats $125k per year. you can grind that out for years or find the next level of scale like trading for clients, another prop firm, or cut a deal with the prop firm you scaled up with.

idk if you're all in on this or its a side-job and you wage but its always about leverage past credibility & performance record to gain greater scale until you manage an uncomfortable amount of money.

62227720
>>62224754
>if the economics are good for one

Well anon, the economics AINT good enough for one hahaha. that's what I'm trying to solve. It's the question I asked in the very first post.

62228083
>>62224207
I am self taught and very well versed in quant the highest sharpe ive achieved is 1.9

62228084
>>62224557
that means hes larping and did the ting

62228111
>>62228084
It feels good to be doubted because what you've done amazes someone. Thanks for giving me the inspiration to keep at it.

62228122
>>62228083
Okay, here's my conundrum then: Suppose you have a list of 10K+ trades spanning from 2020 until now. Each trade has its data attached: date, entry, PnL, and any other metrics that might be important.

How would you go about finding some method or filter to cut out the low-quality trades from the high-quality trades? Ultimately, my strategy relies on momentum, so days where price chops heavily hurt me badly.

Perhaps I could find a way to either identify a chop day before it happens, or find a new addition or advanced criteria that would allow me to dynamically adjust my profit target and stop-loss based on what the model forecasts for that day.

Those are two ideas I have, but I haven’t been able to implement them simply because I’m not sure how to go about it.

I’ve checked the data and found that even the losing trades often have some degree of favorable movement before they eventually lose. That makes me wonder if I could find something that would tell me to reduce my profit target size for that day based on XYZ criteria.

Again, I’m kind of just spitballing because I genuinely don’t know where else to go with this. Everything I’ve tried to improve win rate by cutting losses has inevitably cut out winners as well. So I’m not sure what the right approach is.

62228424
bump

62228603
>>62228122
Low quality and high quality? That’s any MFT Strat lol

62228693
>>62228603
also if i help i get nothing from this

62228705
>>62228603
the strategy is not anything that would require low latency at least to my knowledge since the avg trade lasts anywhere from half an hour to several hours. again, i got no idea where to go from here. any tips would be appreciated

>>62228693
well if you helped me improve it, you could do whatever you want with it. It's not some proprietary strategy that requires some exotic data, it's pretty simple and generic. maybe that's why it's had a minor edge for so long. I would only be trading on it using prop firms, their sim accounts don't trade on live markets. and even if i did, i'm only trading NQ which is hella liquid. And as I've mentioned before, once I make a certain amount of money I'm done for good. Never gonna trade again. In theory if I could get this thing to a workable state, scaling it up would take a few months and I'd hit my goal within a year or two.

62228733
>>62224557
Depends on capital restrictions, an arbitrage or HFT strategy can easily get 1%/day but probably has limits on how much capital it can grow into.

62228952
>>62228733
lol

62228956
add 5% annual IBKR fees itll auto divide for margin or funding then 10 ticks of slippage

62229415
I still have yet to see someone who can help.

>>62228733
1% a day seems impossible in the long run. No strategy can have only good days with no bad days.

>>62228956
If they are buying and selling contracts to such an extent then fees would def eat into it. But if it’s a single buy or sell, or a position that’s held for hours then I don’t see why not.

62229502
Bump

62230495
>>62229415
I coded up orb it doesnt beat fees the original paper is a meme in the algorithmic trading community

try on TQQQ

62230578
>>62230495
if you want to talk serious share discord username

62230627
Based OP, a genuinely original effort thread with potential. Will continue to lurk for interest and hopes to obtain some knowledge, but this is out of scope

62232509
>>62230495
It had basis on ORB and VWAP but isn't just that.

>>62230578
Before any of that I'd like to hear your ideas on what you'd do / suggest for me to improve the strategy. I've already tried a lot of things, so I'd like to know how you could help me.

>>62230627
Thanks, but I haven't really gotten anything good out of this thread yet hahaha

62232338

62232349

>>62232322

62232539
>>62232327
i used to doordash. most of my customers were black. they never tipped. i have a feeling it's not just a black person thing and no one ever tips, though

62232560
>>62232335

62232569
>>62232322
i used to do this with my gf and it was laid back

62232644
>>62232539
once i tipped 6$ and the guy screamed at me as he pulled away thanks for the dollar asshole so idk maybe doordash doesn't do tips right like they say and just wants everyone to act like niggers to each other on purpose

item.title: Will credit cards ever be a viable option for people who aren't debt slaves ever again? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Will credit cards ever be a viable option for people who aren't debt slaves ever again?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-14 22:23 UTC

OP

Will credit cards ever be a viable option for people who aren't debt slaves ever again? All new cards are 20% - 40% APR which is just goy rape.

Early replies (first 3)

62230546
>>62230525

Inflation favors the debtor

62230582
>>62230525
does apr even matter if you pay the balance off every month?

62230803
>>62230582
This. I've never paid a single cent in interest. I honestly found it shocking to realize most people don't just pay the full balance every single month.
I'd say people who don't pay the bill in full each month shouldn't use credit cards, but they fund my reward points and I've got a free round trip business class flight to Germany in September thanks to them so yeah have at it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62232645
>>62230525
Just used it like a debit card and only spend money you have, pay it off right away and enjoy the 5% etc cash back

62230910

62230914

62230921

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231115
p + c, stupid stinkies

PEE PLUS SEE

62231117
>>62231115
its crazy how delicious seeing posts like these are now, every fuddie reply is like music to my ears
chainlink momentum is unstoppable

62231174
>>62231117
im screenshotting your post so that I can show you the link chart 3 months from now and we can discuss whether you were referring to unstoppable upward momentum or unstoppable downward momentum

62231184
>>62231174
do it, it can be as low as $1 and I will be delighted. Keep posting, keep it fresh. you're bound to fud chainlink til you're dead. Shit stays in your mind 24/7 and thats exactly what we want, now stay put you little cuck

62231238
looks like the big boys are getting their compliance ducks in a row before clarity .. are your ducks in a row, anon?

62231247
>>62231184
if it drops to $1 you'll be delighted?
being a cultish fanatic who desires downward momentum for your portfolio isn't a power flex at all

62231282
>>62230995
holy fuck is this real? when was this tweet?

kek the layerzero meltdown was the best marketing Chainlink could have gotten, really
it only costed about 298 milly, paid for by stupid nigger retards (layerzero trusters)

62231305
>>62231184
>do it, it can be as low as $1 and I will be delighted.
coping stinkie

62231365
>>62230995
You get what you fucking pay for

62231382
>>62231117
>chainlink momentum is unstoppable

are you talking about chainlink labs, the company you don't have shares in?


question - when you're sitting here in may of 2027 and the ccip tracker is showing daily fees of $600, what's going to be your excuse?


>>62231184
wow you owned that guy losing money for a decade straight

62231489
>>62231382
You’re late today skinny boy how’s that diet comin along have you put any weight on yet?

62231615
>>62231489
>anybody accidentally kills someone in a fight they go to jail, it's called manslaughter

62231646
>>62231615
you are brown
i am white
i have already won at life hahahahahahahahahahahahaha
you will ALWAYS be brown
i hope you fixed your porn/masturbation addiction though, for real

62231699
>>62230891

Lmao

62231801
>>62231282
https://x.com/LayerZero_Core/status/1996618258066411820

5 months ago.

62231802
i think it might actually be LINK's time

62231853
>>62230891
>>62231282

Lido too now lol
Fresh off the presses

62231868
>>62231843
It was foretold. Obvious in hindsight.

62231901
cuckolds are embarassing lmao

price + chart

62231946
>>62231853
ALL AT ONCE

62231990
>>62231853
oooooooof

62231992
>>62231946
OLD NEWS buddy

62232282
>>62230891

This is actually FUD. Cross Chain Interoperability Protocol is a load of gobbly gook (I skimmed thoroughly) and chainlink still hasn't proven its original use case (solving the problem of getting data into smart contracts in a decentralized manner).

The very concept of Sergey's oracle system is unproven, although sensible. All this bullshit being put on top is not "exciting" or "value-added," it is FUD. It is a stream of bullshit without any actual real world uses.

You retards are getting excited by someone scooping shit on top of technological marvels. Imagine the first airplane, thats a blockchain. Now imagine a woman jumping on top the cockpit and taking a huge dump. Thats what your getting excited about.

62232408
>>62231646
Saar I'm sooo white saar!

62232418
Wait I thought LINK was a SCAM?!? You're telling me the most upstanding, honest, and forthcoming company in crypto, King Kraken, has literally entrusted their reputation in the hands of a fat russian big mac enjoyer? So LINK is not a scam? Somebody comfort me please...

62232437
>>62232418
the scoundrel sergey is very convincing

62232441
>>62230891
Thats cool and all but.... one small problem.... uhh... price??? Oh...... and... chart??

62232461
>>62232408
that's adem newfaggot
lurk moar

62232525
>>62232282

Now X R P. THAT is the one correct thing they are choosing!!

item.title: /smg/ — 418 replies — 56.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/

Open thread • Replies: 418 • Updated: 2026-05-14 15:12 UTC

OP

Real world edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

Old
>>62223487

Early replies (first 3)

62224553
we dont need another thread their is one at 100 now bugger of lad

62224574
>>62224553
This one's going to 420 ATH

62226335
Anon should be recycling these threads. It’s good for the environment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230538
>>62230527
we're just having a bit of fun in here ;)

62230539
>no Trump shitposts while he's on holiday
Sad! I want more wild swings from ramblings with no actual follow through

62230542
>>62230530
check this out

62230545
>>62230532
That's the issue. Inflation could bring rate cuts and then events like the yen carry trade unwinding

62230554
>>62230545
>That's the issue.
Future issue. A whiplash is a given at this point, might as well ride the wave till it comes

62230555
CAM ON SEVENTY FIVE HUNNED

62230556
>>62230527

Unironically might buy some since the low iq’s on stocktwits are openly bag holding

62230557
>>62230527
>are these all the same poster?
Clearly not. I wouldn't touch this hot garbage (American Fusion? Really?) even if it was available on Robinhood.
Penny Meme Stock Pump and Dumps are obvious, but adding country flags to posts would make it even more obvious, Saar!

62230558
Judging my the lack of mentions of my ticker....and someone else complaining about Sandisk....I will not check my port....

62230559
WE DID IT SMIGGIE WIGGIES

7,500 BIG ONES ON SPEE 500

CONGRATULATIONS TO EACH AND EVERY ONE OF YOU

62230561
LET THE BAKERS DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD

62230562
>dow over 50

62230565
>>62230527
>they think they aren't one of us
oh no no hivemind bros

62230568
HULK HOGAN
WE COMIN FO YOU NIGGA

62230572

dumped a stock 20% today because the company is opening a plant in the us

62230573
I am still making money off of sneedisk
thank you for your attention to this matter

62230576
>>62230562

62230577
>>62230562
Yeah, but this time we don't have some useless cunt announcing it (I already fucked her)

62230579

62230581
>>62230572

62230585
>>62230584
WOULD

62230586
>>62230584
PamGODS won

62230587
>>62230576
>>62230584
build it twice as high

62230590

62230591
>>62230581
they had great earnings, and announced expansion. it's not that the earnings were priced in

62230592
>>62230573

62230594
>the /smg/ to end all /smg/‘s

62230596
>>62230595
>>62230595
>>62230595

62230597

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 97 replies — 53.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 97 • Updated: 2026-05-14 20:44 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

62224894
>>62224666
Crazy

62224972
>>62224666
We're cooked

62226444
>>62224666
Risk-on driven by geopolitics, liquidity, and alcoholism. Peak crypto posting.

62228393
>>62216987
>ICP

62228841
chainlink

62229090
>>62216987
he's also pedo

62232214
>>62228393
And why

62232231
>>62195275
>buying the top of the bull trap
Never change, /biz/

62232233
>>62195275
Alts
If I'm already going for a risky meme-asset like crypto I might as well go for broke. Bitcoin already had its big run, now it's just stock market levels of gains with far greater risk.

62232268
>>62232231
Also, this threads riddled with bots holy shit

62232289
>>62195275
Altcoins for profit, Bitcoin for stable investments.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 67 replies — 31.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-05-14 20:25 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228809
>>62225050
Just keep working hard and you're gonna make it anon

62228821
>>62228803
No way in hell am I gonna risk my bitcoin for some pennies on some centralized exchange.

62228833
Btc is Epstein pedo coin…it’s all a con, they were pissing their pants over xrp

Satan coin = BTC
God coin = XRP

62228837
>>62228833
Satan why?

62228839
>>62228821
too risky

62228849
>>62228809
What's the minimum salary for a man

62229042
>>62228821
I think you misunderstood me anon. I don’t like the centralized exchange model either, which is why the self-custody side of BTCFi is way more interesting.

62229048
>>62222489
Why LTC?

62229075
>>62228833
God coin? wake up

62229077
>>62228839
Every platform has risks

62229086
>>62225044
Wait for $1M and have a good life for the rest of your life

62229114
>>62229042
I don't trust "smart" contracts either for holding substantial funds long term. The most I've ever been willing to risk is like $50k on DeFi.

62229336
>>62229114
I know anon, but the newer native BTC infrastructure is built more around predefined protocol rules while still keeping self custody. And nobody’s saying you need to ape your entire stack into it either.

62229340
>>62228391
Sure, poorfag
>>62229086
I lost hope for it, i don't think I'll see that during my lifetime. I'll be lucky to get 300k price wise before 2035 kek.

62229342
>>62220158
He also got decent bags

62229433
>>62229340
Pass it to your children. Hodl

62229475
>>62219942
I will own productive assets instead sar.

62229508
>>62229475
What's that? Is there any?

62229551
>>62229433
I don't have kids, it's over
>>62229508
Sp500 is beating our ass daily, tomorrow is making another ath kek

62229557
>>62229114
Don't trust
>>62229551
You should be having kids if you're over 30 now. Think of your fortune if you have any

62229561
>>62229551
What about forex?

62229570
>>62229557
Yeah, don't think I'm getting kids anytime soon and blew it with one of the few women I might had.
>>62229561
I'm an europoor and i don't do forex trading. I just hold and dca my wagie paycheck, but BTC has been shit this whole market run. And yes the opportunity cost once again is making no sense. Why not pile everything into boomer stocks, better risk and reward after all. I might need to diversify my bags and forget about Bitcoin for a while.
Godspeed

62229578
>>62229570
Be loyal ser and start a good family

62230004
I'm all in StrategyInc tho

62230021
>>62219942
What's supposed to be happening in the op pic

62231066
>>62225044
I got jewed of my own dip, gf was home all day yesterday and i didn't buy.
I hate women so much is unbelievable

62231072
>>62229557
Why though anon? It’s not like this is some random vaporware project. They already have partnerships with names like GoMining, Ledger, a16zcrypto and others around the ecosystem.

62231081
>>62230004
Why all in? : (

62231090
>>62229433
Good advice so far.

62232211
>>62222594
There's solution for this

item.title: PIECE OF SHIT CRYPTO — 18 replies — 11.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
PIECE OF SHIT CRYPTO

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-14 21:01 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62228142
really dogshit at this point, even pokemon cards and ACTUAL POTATOES are doing better than btc.

62228149
I got rich by buying silver

62228162
I'll still make a profit if i could go back to 2011 and not spend those 70 bitcoins i bought with only 10% transaction fee from that based guido who had the best fuckin rates.
Back then people used ti charge you 25% or 30% for buying a btc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228166
crypto is going to go sideways for 20 years. Too many normgroids got in and all the og holders don't believe in it anymore

62228315
>>62228166

62228322
>>62228166
>all the og holders don't believe in it anymore
what about Max Keiser?

62228328
>>62228166
normgroids are gobbling up US stocks now

62228420
this is a good thing. bitcoin is supposed to counter-establishment so it should move against boomer index

62228426
My investments are up 7,000 USD today.

62229082
>>62228104
Ethers gravity is strong, it ll dumo further.....

62229102
>>62228166
No, a new narrative will happen soon and crypto will moon out of nowhere

62229360
>>62228104
See >>62225359

The top 10 is full of useless scams, while the key to actual crypto mass adoption is barely in the top 15.
This crypto market is all scammers, manipulators, and retards. Value investors are staying far away.

Until Link is rank 3, crypto will stay dead and stocks will continue to pump.

62229403
>>62228104
It literally doesn't do anything, or solve any problem.

Gambling on stocks is very easy now, and they've just got rid of the pattern day trader rule.

62229444
>>62229360
cope linkchud

62229465
>>62229360
>until LINK is rank 3
Link was rank 5 nearly 6 years ago stupid baggie

62229580
>>62229444
>>62229465
The stock market actually tends to price in news (bad and good), and tends to uphold relatively accurate market cap rankings based on current fundamentals and future potential.

The crypto market does not, so why would anyone invest?

62232288
>>62229102
crypto is fully over
No one wants to be their own bank when your wallet and operating system are coded by microjeets in the age of AI and quantum computing

62232348
>>62228149
That's basically impossible. Even if you stacked in 1965 before the end of silver money in the U.S., you could only make a 60X. But you didn't do that because you're a zoomer who was born too late to make more than a 12X.

My BTC has done a 270X since 2014.

item.title: This is getting ridiculous — 36 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is getting ridiculous

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-05-14 18:06 UTC

OP

MSFT is currently trading at January 2024 prices. That’s 2.5 years of zero gains. Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world and growing in the high teens every year. I can’t see how this isn’t a screaming buy right now. I recognize the uncertainly surrounding their investment into AI, and possibly the negative sentiment around software stocks in general but for me Microsoft isn’t going anywhere

Early replies (first 3)

62228288
>>62228282
i bought around $400. i also bought META yesterday.

MA and V are good buys now also.

62228292
>>62228282
I have an average price per share of $366 I just keep buying along with Amazon Google and Apple.

62228297
>>62228288
V is a screaming buy but I don’t know enough about stable coins to assess the proper risk to the credit card business yet.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228321
>>62228309
Yessir

62228343
windows is the boomer os thats being jeeted to hell, and everyone can see the writing on the wall. its like everytime a new windows version drops the screams of dissatisfaction get louder. Thats what happens after 20 years of letting indians nepo hire more of their kind. everyone is leaving azure for AWS too. gen alpha tr00ns will all be vibecoding on mac minis and lenovos running fedora. think of how many PowerBI boomers are retiring in the next decade. why even bother with x86 when the day of the pillow finally arrives and frees us all.

62228415
>>62228297
A company trading at a PE of around 28 is screaming buy? It's only down like 10% in the last year.

62228438
>>62228282
The only thing Microsoft does well is datacenter hosting. Everything else they do is shit.

They make their gains through shady business practices like dark patterns and built in spyware or buying out and shutting down the competition. Not by having a best in class product and marketing it well.

You need to take all of those things into consideration before going long on MSFT.

62228448
Reason the share price is stagnant is cuz all of the people laid off there got their RSUs early and have cashed out.

62228452
The market (chiefly AI) is fully saturated with all the monopoly money anyone is currently willing to risk. Things have to start dying for other things to achieve "fair value"

62228457
>>62228282
Culmination of Azure getting mogged by AWS and ChatGPT (Microsoft is balls deep in OpenAI) losing ground to Gemini and Claude.

62228475
>>62228415
They are a digital payment toll keeper and the bridge is already built a small amount of maintenance capital expenditure per year and you are set.

62228486
>>62228282
If AI fails to boost economic growth as much as the markets expect then tech stocks crash but if it does then software loses value.
MSFT might be worth buying starting at around 200-250. Currently indexes are overheated on the HTFs which makes long-term investments risky.

62228488
>>62228415
one day /biz/ will learn to look at the 1 year forward PE and growth rate

62228489
>>62228486
its already boosting economic growth

62228502
>>62228282
>Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world
their main product is worthless
you don't need any office products, you can interact with them online/ through a FOSS or through your agent/LLM
you also don't make them yourself anymore

microsaar has been dumping money into openAI but they will not be getting any equity
they're on track to become IBM 2

62228524
>>62228489
Real GDP YoY isn't spiking at all. Should be like 5% to justify the parabolic market rally.

62228537
>>62228282
>5% linkedin layoffs
>20-30% of some teams wiped company wide
>ticker didn't even twitch
lmao macroshart

62228596
Designated shitting company

62228906
Microsoft is history. We are going to see at least 80%-90% downside from here. Nothing is going to save this.

62228910
Also notice Nadella wasn’t invited to China. He’s the biggest embarrassment in the tech sector.

62228937
>>62228282
idk pal, if that was a coin would you buy this chart?

62229462
>>62228282
So well positioned after turning their OS into a Linux bootloader with extra spyware and renaming their flagship Office products after a shit chatbot and making them rolling updates so no longer stable either as UI or backwards compatibility.
Do they still do the Xbox thing?
Only positive I can find is that the pivot to AI will optimize their enshittification workload and remove billions of jeets but I don't know how that can be monetized.

62229815
>>62228282
It will inevitably swing upwards unless the AI bubble collapses, but in that case you wouldn't want to hold any other stocks anyway.
Or you could just put everything in Nebius, Sivers and Silicon Motion if you're convinced the AI boom will persist.

62229845
>>62228910
Wasn't he called up to testify in the OpenAI lawsuit the other day?
He probably doesn't appreciate missing out on that while Elon schmoozes with Xi and Trump.

62229958
>>62228282
this company is completely jeeted and its only a matter of time when people stop trusting it, other companies need security most and trusting jeets with security is a big gamble, my prediction is that many companies working with MS will be looking for alternatives.

62230475
>>62228282
> Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world
Lol. Lmao

Maybe if they purge the browns they could be.

62230486
>>62228448
>3T market cap
>a couple million in RSUs make a dent in the price

62230504
>>62228282
It's been jeeted from top to bottom and it's on its way to becoming the next IBM.

62230507
It's ridiculous because you suffered opportunity cost

It's a screaming buy because you still own it

62230521
>>62228282
sold my small position last week. its going nowhere. its fairly priced right now because its a dog shit company.

62231411
>>62230486
You don't know what market cap actually is, do you?
>a couple million in RSUs make a dent in the price
Yes. That's exactly how stock market works. A bunch of profiteers push the price up and down with their millions while you see clickbait headlines like 500 gazillion dollars wiped out from the market.

62231427
>>62231411
He can't even imagine what it would be like to not have breakfast this morning, expecting him to understand price action is a stretch.

62231571
>>62228282
they are losing market share to opensource year after year with their horrible products, and are actively pushing for subscription based software which nobody ever wants or will buy.
use your brain.
